summary:
Claim: Sugar restriction improved survival in individuals exposed to poor hygiene.  
Evidence: - The Vipeholm studies (mid-20th century) showed lower mortality in subjects with limited sugar intake under unsanitary conditions. - High sugar intake promoted harmful gut microbes, weakening immune defenses against frequent infections.  
Answer: C
